overview:
## Overview

Adding a [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKSKNodeComponent] object to an entity automatically updates the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/SpriteKit/SKNode/entity] property of the component’s SpriteKit node (an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/SpriteKit/SKNode] object) to point to that entity.

When you add entities and components to a node in the Xcode SpriteKit scene editor, Xcode automatically creates a [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKSKNodeComponent] object to manage the relationship between that SpriteKit node and the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKEntity] object that node represents. Load the scene file with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKScene] class to access these entities and components.

For more information on Entity-Component architecture, read [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/EntityComponent.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172-CH6] in [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

# GKSKNodeComponent

**Class**

A component that manages a SpriteKit node.

## Declaration

```swift
class GKSKNodeComponent
```

## Overview

Adding a [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKSKNodeComponent] object to an entity automatically updates the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/SpriteKit/SKNode/entity] property of the component’s SpriteKit node (an [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/SpriteKit/SKNode] object) to point to that entity.

When you add entities and components to a node in the Xcode SpriteKit scene editor, Xcode automatically creates a [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKSKNodeComponent] object to manage the relationship between that SpriteKit node and the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKEntity] object that node represents. Load the scene file with the [doc://com.apple.gameplaykit/documentation/GameplayKit/GKScene] class to access these entities and components.



For more information on Entity-Component architecture, read [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/EntityComponent.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172-CH6] in [https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Conceptual/GameplayKit_Guide/index.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40015172].

## Creating a SpriteKit Component

- **init(node:)**: Initializes a component to manage the specified SpriteKit node.

## Accessing the Component’s SpriteKit Node

- **node**: The SpriteKit node managed by the component.

## Xcode and SpriteKit Integration

- **GKScene**: A container for associating GameplayKit objects with a SpriteKit scene.
- **GKSceneRootNodeType**: Identifies scene classes from other frameworks that support embedded GameplayKit information.

## Inherits From

- GKComponent

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- GKAgentDelegate
- Hashable
- NSCoding
- NSCopying
- NSObjectProtocol
- NSSecureCoding
